Aura3D quick patterns for AI coding agents.

Scaffold:
```bash
npx create-aura3d@latest my-scene --template product-viewer
cd my-scene
npx @aura3d/cli@latest assets add ./assets/robot.glb --name robot
npm run dev
```

Hello world:
```ts
import { createAuraApp, scene, model, lights } from "@aura3d/engine";
import { assets } from "./aura-assets";

createAuraApp("#app", {
  scene: scene().add(model(assets.robot)).add(lights.studio())
});
```

Public imports:
```ts
import {
  createAuraApp,
  collectAuraSceneEvidence,
  definePromptPlan,
  compilePromptPlan,
  promptPlanToScene,
  scene,
  model,
  camera,
  lights,
  material,
  effects,
  prefabs,
  primitives,
  group,
  groups,
  shadows,
  timeline,
  interactions,
  physics,
  labels,
  environments,
  games,
  charts,
  character,
  city,
  defineAuraAssets,
  ui
} from "@aura3d/engine";
```

Physics API boundary:
- Prefer `prefabs.physicsPlayground(...)`, `prefabs.physicsRamp()`, and
  `prefabs.miniGolfHole()` for benchmark-visible physics evidence.
- Use the safe root `physics` namespace from `@aura3d/engine` for simulation
  state: `physics.world(...)`, `physics.body(...)`, `physics.box(...)`,
  `physics.sphere(...)`, `physics.step(...)`, `physics.debug(...)`, and
  `physics.debugNodes(...)`.
- Use `physics.worldFromScene(scene)` when you authored nodes with
  `.physics(...)` and want Aura3D to create bodies/colliders from the scene.
- Do not write `import { PhysicsWorld, Shape, PhysicsDebugAdapter } from
  "@aura3d/engine"`. Those are not agent-facing root imports; use the namespace
  helpers instead of inventing root symbols.

Typed asset rule:
- Use `model(assets.robot)` from generated `src/aura-assets.ts`.
- Do not use `model("robot")`; the safe API rejects string asset ids.
- Do not invent asset paths. Run `assets add`, then read the generated module.
- Use `unsafeModelUrl(...)` only as an explicit temporary escape hatch.

Common scenes:
```ts
const plan = definePromptPlan({
  sceneType: "product-viewer",
  subject: { asset: assets.product },
  camera: { preset: "product-orbit" },
  lighting: { preset: "studio-softbox" },
  effects: ["bloom"],
  interaction: "orbit",
  acceptanceCriteria: ["product centered", "studio lighting visible"]
} as const);

const compiled = compilePromptPlan(plan);
createAuraApp("#app", { scene: promptPlanToScene(plan) });
console.log(compiled.report.visualSystems, compiled.report.repairHints);
```

Lower-level scene helpers remain available:
```ts
scene()
  .background("#08111f")
  .add(model(assets.product).scale(1.1))
  .add(lights.studio({ intensity: 1.2 }))
  .add(interactions.orbit())
  .camera(camera.orbit({ distance: 4 }));
```

`createAuraApp("#app", ...)` gives a direct empty app container viewport-safe
layout by default. Agent-authored benchmark scenes do not need custom CSS just
to remove browser body margin or make the canvas fill the screenshot.
`createAuraApp` also accepts nullable `document.querySelector(...)` results and
throws a clear runtime error if the target is missing, so nested mount helper
functions can stay TypeScript-safe.
Use `collectAuraSceneEvidence(scene)` or route diagnostics to report physics
body/collider counts, interaction modes, follow/orbit camera state, animation
clips, collision-aware labels, helper performance budgets, and typed asset
provenance. Use `character.visualQA(nodes)` to catch disconnected primitive
limbs or impossible proportions before accepting humanoid screenshots.

```ts
scene()
  .background("#05070d")
  .add(model(assets.hero).position(0, 0, -1))
  .add(lights.point({ position: [-2, 2.4, 1], color: "#38d6ff", intensity: 2.4 }))
  .add(effects.rain({ intensity: 0.45 }))
  .add(effects.fog({ density: 0.18 }))
  .add(effects.bloom({ intensity: 0.42 }))
  .camera(camera.dolly({ from: [0, 1.4, 6], to: [0, 1.2, 2.2], seconds: 8 }))
  .timeline(timeline.loop({ seconds: 8 }));
```

Round 1 repair patterns:
```ts
// Physics playgrounds need renderable scene evidence; do not build only a
// custom 2D canvas around the physics package. The prefab includes falling
// cubes, settled cubes, contact patches, normal vectors, and gravity cues.
scene()
  .background("#070b12")
  .addMany(prefabs.physicsPlayground({ cubes: 50 }))
  .add(lights.studio({ intensity: 1.15 }))
  .camera(camera.physics());

// Real dense particle system, not a symbolic cone, single curve, or HUD counter.
// Add a real emission-rate control for the benchmark prompt; label-only
// buttons do not count.
scene()
  .background("#030711")
  .addMany(prefabs.particleFountain({ count: 2400, emissionRate: 120 }))
  .add(lights.studio({ intensity: 1.15 }))
  .camera(camera.autoFrame({ bounds: { min: [-2.4, 0, -2.4], max: [2.4, 3.4, 2.4] } }));

// City prompts need streets, scale, windows, crosswalks, street lights,
// storefront detail, foreground sun/moon day-night markers, and readable
// framing. If there is a day/night button, it must rebuild or update the
// scene's sky, lighting, windows, street lights, and state marker.
scene()
  .background("#061018")
  .addMany(prefabs.cityBlock({ blocks: 20, litWindows: true, timeOfDay: "night" }))
  .add(effects.fog({ density: 0.045 }))
  .add(effects.bloom({ intensity: 0.18 }))
  .camera(camera.city());

// Solar-system prompts need six planets, orbit paths, labels, sun bloom, and
// whole-system framing.
scene()
  .background("#020617")
  .addMany(prefabs.solarSystem({ labels: "attached", orbitSegments: 24, starCount: 42 }))
  .add(lights.studio({ intensity: 0.85 }))
  .camera(camera.solar());

// Neon tunnel prompts need an actual inside-the-tube flythrough: foreground
// rings receding to a vanishing point, perspective rails, floor reflections,
// fog, bloom, particles, and a dolly camera. Do not submit a flat gradient, one
// portal, rectangular box, or CSS-only animation.
scene()
  .background("#020617")
  .addMany(prefabs.neonTunnel({ rings: 24 }))
  .camera(camera.neon())
  .timeline(timeline.loop({ seconds: 8 }));

// Material labs need distinct material classes and contrast/reflection cues.
scene()
  .background("#10151f")
  .addMany(prefabs.materialSwatches())
  .add(lights.studio({ intensity: 1.55 }))
  .camera(camera.materials());

// 3D data visualization prompts should use renderable bar geometry once: bars,
// caps, base shadows, grid rails, wall ticks, labels, grounded legend, and
// orbit controls. Add DOM title/axis/tick/readout text around the Aura canvas;
// primitive label chips alone are not enough. Do not dispose/recreate the app
// in a requestAnimationFrame loop or replace the scene with a flat DOM chart.
scene()
  .background("#071017")
  .addMany(prefabs.dataBars3D({ grid: 6 }))
  .add(lights.studio({ intensity: 1.1 }))
  .add(interactions.orbit())
  .camera(camera.charts());

// Product viewers should use the typed one-call product viewer helper plus a
// plinth, contact shadow, softboxes, and orbit/turntable cues.
scene()
  .addMany(prefabs.productViewer(assets.product))
  .add(interactions.orbit())
  .camera(camera.product())
  .timeline(timeline.loop({ seconds: 8 }));

// Physics demos must expose real state/cues, not cosmetic falling boxes only.
scene()
  .addMany(prefabs.physicsRamp())
  .add(lights.studio())
  .camera(camera.physics());
```

Templates:
- `product-viewer`: product or asset inspection scenes.
- `cinematic-scene`: camera path, lighting, rain/fog/bloom scenes.
- `mini-game`: primitive interactive game slice.

Visual acceptance:
- Benchmark prompts: read `docs/agents/benchmark-recipes.md` and copy the
  smallest matching recipe before writing custom scene logic.
- For benchmark runs, do not run `npm run dev`, `npm run preview`, Playwright,
  browser screenshot capture, or manual visual verification inside the agent
  process. Run finite commands such as `npm install` and `npm run build`, then
  stop and report the build/run commands.
- Do not build custom physics, game, chart, material, city, or animation
  engines when a matching `prefabs.*` helper exists.
- Use prompt plans and recipe scenes before custom primitive placement.
- A nonblank screenshot is not enough. The image must visibly match the prompt.
- Reject one-asset-plus-symbolic-effect output as product-quality proof.
- For particles, use `effects.particles(...)` or `prefabs.particleFountain(...)`;
  do not draw an emitter cone and claim particles exist. For the fountain
  prompt, add a real emission-rate input/control and frame upward/falling arcs,
  lifetime color variation, nozzle, and ground collision/splash evidence.
- For city, solar-system, material, product, and physics prompts, start from the matching
  `prefabs.*` helper before custom primitive placement.
- For mini-golf, use `prefabs.miniGolfHole()` with `camera.follow({ targetNode:
  "white physics golf ball" })`, `games.createMiniGolfState()`, and a visible
  score HUD. Keep the aim line, shot-power meter, ball trail, contact shadow,
  obstacle contact flash, and cup in frame.
- For data-viz, mini-game, tunnel, and humanoid prompts, start from
  `prefabs.dataBars3D`, `prefabs.miniGolfHole`, `prefabs.neonTunnel`, and
  `prefabs.primitiveHumanoid({ showJoints: true, motionTrail: true })`; the
  humanoid prefab includes connected body parts, joint hinges, path cues, face
  details, non-detached stride ribbons, root bob, joint-hierarchy limb swing,
  and a `walk` animation clip that keeps primitive limbs connected for
  screenshots.
- For small HUD text, counters, buttons, and sliders, use `ui.html`,
  `ui.setText`, `ui.onClick`, `ui.range`, and `ui.onInput`. Do not use `HTMLStrongElement` or untyped
  `event.currentTarget` in benchmark TypeScript.
- Do not call `dispose()` and `createAuraApp()` every animation frame. Create
  one app, use `.animate(...)`, `timeline.loop(...)`, or update ordinary DOM
  overlays separately.
- For benchmark work, write the smallest complete Aura scene first, run finite
  commands like `npm run build`, and exit. Do not keep `npm run dev` attached to
  the agent process.
- For product-viewer asset prompts, first run
  `npx @aura3d/cli@latest assets add ./assets/product.glb --name product`, read
  `src/aura-assets.ts`, and use `model(assets.product)`. Do not use string ids,
  invented URLs, or `unsafeModelUrl(...)` for benchmark product proof.
- If visual review fails, apply `compilePromptPlan(plan).report.repairHints`
  before changing the scene or review label.

Verification:
```bash
npm run build
npm run test
npx @aura3d/cli@latest assets validate
npx @aura3d/cli@latest check-deploy
```

Agent docs:
- `docs/agents/README.md`
- `docs/agents/api-surface.md`
- `docs/agents/asset-workflow.md`
- `docs/agents/templates.md`
- `docs/agents/deployment.md`
- `docs/agents/troubleshooting.md`
- `docs/agents/anti-hallucination-rules.md`

Non-goals:
- Aura3D is not the AI model.
- Aura3D does not source, license, or host a universal asset catalog.
- Aura3D does not eval arbitrary generated code in the browser.
- Source code is the scene description; provider JSON is not the public authoring API.
